title: HBO Max Isn't TV—It's Movie Paradise. Here Are 25 You Should Watch.
format: Amanda's off, so Sean Fennessey walks the just-launched HBO Max movie catalog with Bobby Wagner: the TCM and Warner vault, Criterion, Studio Ghibli, the sports and music docs. He hands out pairings to start with, then talks with documentarians Kirby Dick and Amy Ziering about On the Record.

segments.heading: What's actually on HBO Max
segments.note: the buckets: TCM, Warner vault, DC, Criterion, Ghibli, originals

films.title: Casablanca
films.note: Sean's example of the Warner/TCM vault: watch this, King Kong, and Rebel Without a Cause in one night without switching services.

films.title: Dirty Harry
films.note: Cited here as a vault title; recommended again at the end as Clint Eastwood pre-gaming before next week's 90th-birthday episode.

films.title: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre
Aquaman
films.note: Part of the DC Universe catalog that lands here under the Warner umbrella.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: The 'Snyder Cut,' announced for the service the week before. Sean and Amanda argue the year-out timing is free publicity, not a mistake.

films.title: Spirited Away
films.note: The whole Studio Ghibli library arrives on streaming in the States for the first time. Sean calls this Miyazaki's best and pairs it with The Wizard of Oz; Bobby puts it first in his queue.

films.title: Ponyo
films.note: One of the Ghibli titles Sean hasn't seen yet and is looking forward to.

films.title: Toy Story
films.note: Not on the service, invoked to make the Pixar-owes-Ghibli case. John Lasseter was a big Miyazaki fan; watch Spirited Away right after this.

films.title: Let Them All Talk
films.note: Among the thin slate of HBO Max originals: the new Steven Soderbergh, form still unknown.

films.title: An American Pickle
films.note: A Point Grey / Seth Rogen picture HBO Max picked up; sounds like a period comedy.

films.title: Superintelligence
films.note: A Melissa McCarthy comedy directed by her husband Ben Falcone, bumped from last year under the Warner banner.

films.title: Charm City Kings
films.note: Picked up out of festivals last year.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Netflix reference points for the same question: will people tune in for a streaming movie?

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: This week's news: the DiCaprio/Scorsese adaptation of the David Grann book is going to Apple, distributed theatrically by Paramount, the same everyone's-a-streamer strategy.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: The Disney+ comparison: a deep catalog of nostalgia you have access to but don't need to watch a fourth time.

segments.heading: Sean's picks: where to start, with pairings
segments.note: Criterion essentials, samurai, sports docs, music movies, three he's saving

films.title: The Red Shoes
films.note: The Powell and Pressburger backstage ballet tragedy. Sean just rewatched it and pairs it with Whiplash as a dynamite double feature about the cost of being great.

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: The Damien Chazelle / Miles Teller film, one of his favorites of the last 20 years and the modern-day companion to The Red Shoes.

films.title: Armageddon
films.note: Once, improbably, a member of the Criterion Collection, never reissued on Blu-ray. Sean will defend it; you know where he stands on Michael Bay.

films.title: Sword of Vengeance
films.note: The first Lone Wolf and Cub film, 84 minutes, a post-Kurosawa samurai movie whose shogun-executioner-and-child setup reads a lot like The Mandalorian and Baby Yoda.

films.title: The Killing
films.note: Early Kubrick heist picture with Sterling Hayden. Sean's pairing for fans of Heat.

films.title: Heat
films.note: The Rewatchables touchstone that sets up the pairing with The Killing.

films.title: Network
films.note: The Sidney Lumet / Paddy Chayefsky movie: prescient, funny, strange, lots of people yelling, which Bobby notes Sean is fond of.

films.title: The Long Goodbye
films.note: Robert Altman's 70s noir with Elliott Gould. Sean floats doing a whole episode on it.

films.title: Hoop Dreams
films.note: The doc that taught Sean a sports movie isn't about sports; he'd just paid for it a la carte before it hit streaming.

films.title: When We Were Kings
films.note: Raised in the sports-doc Mount Rushmore run, via a Bill Simmons podcast conversation.

films.title: O.J.: Made in America
films.note: 'Watched it last week.' Very powerful, and otherwise an ESPN+ exclusive, now here.

films.title: Senna
films.note: Asif Kapadia's portrait of the race car driver.

films.title: Icarus
films.note: Starts like a sports movie and turns into a true-crime story, increasingly the order of the day.

films.title: The Endless Summer
films.note: The great surfing hybrid documentary.

films.title: Beyond the Mat
films.note: The late-90s pro-wrestling doc that pulled back the curtain; hard to be a fan after it, Sean says, but very effective.

films.title: Shall We Dance
films.note: One of the Warner musicals in the deep music-movie collection.

films.title: Wattstax
films.note: Among the concert films on the service.

films.title: Monterey Pop
films.note: Which Liz Hannah talked up on the Criterion Channel episode.

films.title: Elvis: That's the Way It Is
films.note: About Elvis's 70s comeback tour; Sean hasn't seen it since he was a kid.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: All versions are here, including the Bradley Cooper / Lady Gaga one, a Big Picture Hall of Famer.

films.title: The Sea of Grass
films.note: First of three lesser-known classics Sean's saving to watch, this one from Elia Kazan.

films.title: Countdown
films.note: One of Robert Altman's earliest films, a pre-M*A*S*H space story with James Caan.

films.title: Keeper of the Flame
films.note: The George Cukor Hepburn-and-Tracy picture. Nearly all their movies are on the service.

films.title: Pat and Mike
films.note: Another Hepburn-and-Tracy title in the vault.

films.title: His Girl Friday
films.note: Covered earlier in the week; cited for the pitter-patter you find times a thousand in the Hepburn/Tracy films.

segments.heading: Kirby Dick and Amy Ziering on 'On the Record'

films.title: On the Record
films.note: Their new documentary on abuse of power in the music industry, tethered to the Russell Simmons allegations and centered on Drew Dixon. Oprah and Harpo withdrew and Apple TV+ pulled out days before Sundance; HBO Max picked it up after critics responded strongly to the premiere.

films.title: The Hunting Ground
films.note: Their earlier film on campus sexual assault. Its reputation opened doors, and it's where Ziering first crossed paths with Rose McGowan.

films.title: The Invisible War
films.note: Their Academy Award-nominated doc on assault in the military, cited as the through-line of their organic, subject-first method.

films.title: Investigation of a Citizen Above Suspicion
films.note: Kirby Dick's pick for the last great thing he saw: Elio Petri's Italian film on fascism and police power, which he found staggering.

referenced.group: Whole franchises & filmographies on the service

30 for 30 (ESPN series): No Crossover: The Trial of Allen Iverson
Winning Time: Reggie Miller vs. The New York Knicks
